---
title: "Two-sample t-test - Test epsilon rule for single tests"
date: "Compiled at `r format(Sys.time(), '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', tz = 'UTC')` UTC"
output:
  github_document: default
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
params:
  name: "04_single_tests" # change if you rename file
---

```{r here, message=FALSE}
here::i_am(paste0(params$name, ".Rmd"), uuid = "5e646f9e-bea7-4678-8776-a33608cb3fad")
```

```{r global-options, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  dev = c("png", "cairo_pdf"),
  fig.ext = c("png", "pdf"),
  fig.width = 10, 
  fig.height = 6, 
  dpi = 200,
  fig.path = paste0('figures/', params, "/expl_single_"),
  message = FALSE, 
  warning = FALSE,
  echo = FALSE)
```


In this script we evaluate the performance of the **Standardized Lifted Log-Saturation (SLLS) rule** in the **single-test setting**. Here, each test is treated independently: the $p$-value approximation is computed per test, and the choice of $\varepsilon$ depends only on the test’s own permutation distribution rather than on the maximum statistic across multiple tests. This setup isolates the behavior of the rule in a clean, controlled scenario and allows us to study its properties without the additional complexity introduced by multiple testing.


```{r packages, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load packages
library(conflicted)

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(pheatmap)
library(tibble)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)
library(future)
library(future.apply)
library(kableExtra)
library(furrr)
library(patchwork)

# For permApprox:
library(fdrtool)
library(fitdistrplus)
library(future)
library(future.apply)
library(goftest)
library(limma)
library(progressr)

conflicts_prefer(dplyr::select, dplyr::filter)
```

```{r directories}
# create or *empty* the target directory, used to write this file's data: 
#projthis::proj_create_dir_target(params$name, clean = TRUE)

# function to get path to target directory: path_target("sample.csv")
path_target <- projthis::proj_path_target(params$name)

# function to get path to previous data: path_source("00-import", "sample.csv")
path_source <- projthis::proj_path_source(params$name)
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load permApprox functions
proj_path <- rprojroot::find_root(rprojroot::has_file("README.md"))
permApprox_path <- file.path(proj_path, "functions/permApprox_eps_fct/R")
permApprox_functions <- list.files(permApprox_path, pattern = "\\.R$", full.names = TRUE)
invisible(sapply(permApprox_functions, source))
```

## Data Simulation

```{r}
## Data Simulation with manual t-statistic computation
set.seed(42)

n_tests <- 1000    # number of independent tests
n_per_group_vec <- c(50, 100, 250, 500, 750, 1000) # sample size per group
B <- 1000          # permutations

# Effect sizes by block
effect_blocks <- c(0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2)
delta_vec <- rep(effect_blocks, each = n_tests / length(effect_blocks)) %>%
  factor(levels = effect_blocks)
```

We reuse the data from the former simulation study, where we simulated two groups from normal distributions with increasing effect sizes (difference in means).

The parameters are set as follows:

* Sample sizes per group: `r n_per_group_vec`  
* Number of tests: `r n_tests`  
* Effect sizes (difference in mean): `r paste(effect_blocks, collapse = " ")`  
* Standard deviation: 1  
* Tests per effect size: `r paste0("d=", effect_blocks, ": ",table(delta_vec), collapse = ", ")`

The observed $t$-statistic is calculated as

$$
t_{\text{obs}} = \frac{\bar{x}_1 - \bar{x}_2}
{\sqrt{\frac{s_1^2 + s_2^2}{n}}}
$$

where  
- $\bar{x}_1$ and $\bar{x}_2$ are the sample means,  
- $s_1^2$ and $s_2^2$ are the sample variances, and  
- $n$ is the sample size in each group.


## Plot function

```{r}
plot_pvals <- function(df, sampsize, ylim = NULL, log_scale = TRUE) {
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = obs_stats)) +
    # t-test p-values in black
    geom_point(
      aes(y = p_ttest, shape = "t-test"),
      color = "black", size = 2, stroke = 0.5, alpha = 0.5
    ) +
    # Constrained GPD p-values colored by effect size
    geom_point(
      aes(y = p_constr, shape = "constrained GPD", color = effect_size),
      size = 1.6, alpha = 0.6
    ) +
    scale_shape_manual(
      name = "P-value type:",
      values = c("constrained GPD" = 16, "t-test" = 1)
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
      name = "Effect size:",
      values = c(
        "0"   = "gold",
        "0.5" = "#ffb347", # light orange
        "1"   = "#ff8c00", # orange
        "1.5" = "#e55b00", # deep orange
        "2"   = "red"
      )
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0( "t-test vs. constrained GPD fit (n = ", sampsize, ")"),
      x = expression(abs(t[obs])),
      y = expression(p)
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text       = element_text(size = 14),
      axis.title      = element_text(size = 16),
      plot.title      = element_text(size = 16),
      strip.text      = element_text(size = 15),
      legend.title    = element_text(size = 14),
      legend.text     = element_text(size = 12)    
      #legend.position = "top",
      #legend.box = "vertical"
    )
  
  if (log_scale) {
    p <- p + 
      scale_y_log10(
        breaks = c(1e-300, 1e-200, 1e-100, 1e-80, 1e-60, 1e-40, 1e-20, 1e-2),
        labels = c("1e-300", "1e-200", "1e-100", "1e-80", "1e-60", "1e-40", "1e-20", "1e-2"),
        minor_breaks = NULL
      )
  }
  
  if (!is.null(ylim)) {
    p <- p + coord_cartesian(ylim = ylim)
  }
  
  p <- p + expand_limits(x = 0)
  
  return(p)
}
```

## Function to run permApprox

This function will be used for all p-values approximations with constrained GPD fit.

```{r run_permApprox_function}
run_permApprox <- function(rule_name, eps_fct, constraint = "support_at_obs") {
  for (n_per_group in n_per_group_vec) {
    # Load simulated data
    obs_stats <- readRDS(path_source("03_find_eps_rule",
                                     paste0("obs_t_stats_n", 
                                            n_per_group, "_B", B, ".rds")))
    perm_stats <- readRDS(path_source("03_find_eps_rule",
                                      paste0("perm_t_stats_n", 
                                             n_per_group, "_B", B, ".rds")))
    pval_ttest <- readRDS(path_source("03_find_eps_rule",
                                      paste0("pval_ttest_n", 
                                             n_per_group, "_B", B, ".rds")))
    
    fname_constr <- path_target(paste0("single_test_constr_", rule_name, "_n", 
                                       n_per_group, "_B", B, ".rds"))
    
    if (!file.exists(fname_constr)) {
      cat("n:", n_per_group, "\n")
      
      gpd_ctrl_constr <- make_gpd_ctrl(
        fit_method = "LME",
        tol = 1e-7,
        include_obs = FALSE,
        constraint = constraint,
        thresh_method = "ftr",
        exceed0 = 250,
        exceed_min = 10,
        thresh_step = 10,
        gof_test = "ad",
        gof_alpha = 0.05,
        cores = 1,
        verbose = TRUE
      )
      
      papprox_list <- list()
      
      for (m in 1:n_tests) {
        if (m%%50 == 0) print(m)
    
        papprox_list[[m]] <- papprox_constr <- compute_p_values(
          obs_stats = obs_stats[m],
          perm_stats = perm_stats[, m],
          eps_fct = eps_fct,
          sampsize = n_per_group,
          alternative = "two_sided",
          method = "gpd",
          gpd_ctrl = gpd_ctrl_constr,
          adjust_method = "BH"
        )
      }
      
      saveRDS(papprox_list, file = fname_constr)
    }
  }
  
  # ---- Combine results for all n_per_group values ----
  results_all <- purrr::map_dfr(n_per_group_vec, function(n_per_group) {
    
    # Load the original stats & t-test p-values (same source path as in the loop above)
    obs_stats  <- readRDS(path_source("03_find_eps_rule",
                                      paste0("obs_t_stats_n",  n_per_group, "_B", B, ".rds")))
    pval_ttest <- readRDS(path_source("03_find_eps_rule",
                                      paste0("pval_ttest_n",   n_per_group, "_B", B, ".rds")))
    
    # Load the per-test permApprox results (matches the save name above)
    papprox_list <- readRDS(path_target(
      paste0("single_test_constr_", rule_name, "_n", n_per_group, "_B", B, ".rds")
    ))
    
    # Safety check
    stopifnot(length(papprox_list) == length(obs_stats),
              length(pval_ttest)   == length(obs_stats))
    
    # Build tibble for this n by extracting from each per-test result
    purrr::map_dfr(seq_along(papprox_list), function(m) {
      fit <- papprox_list[[m]]
      
      tibble::tibble(
        idx         = m,
        n_per_group = n_per_group,
        effect_size = delta_vec[m],
        obs_stats   = abs(obs_stats[m]),         # keep absolute t
        p_ttest     = pval_ttest[m],
        gpd_fit     = ifelse(is.null(fit$gpd_fit), NA, fit$gpd_fit$fitted),
        p_constr     = fit$p_unadjusted,
        epsilon     = ifelse(is.null(fit$gpd_fit), NA, fit$gpd_fit$epsilon)
      )
    })
  })
  
  return(results_all)
}
```


## Run permApprox with the SLLS rule for $\varepsilon$

We use the SLLS (Standardized Lifted Log-Saturation) rule to define epsilon and run permApprox with this rule.

```{r, echo=TRUE}
# Build T_cap from choice: "perm" or "tmax"
# - cap_base = "tmax": uses observed maximum across tests
# - cap_base = "perm": uses high quantile of |perm_stats| inflated by g(n)
.build_Tcap <- function(cap_base, t_obs, perm_stats, n, 
                        q_ref = 0.99, alpha = 0.5) {
  if (identical(cap_base, "tmax")) {
    # Observed cap (same for all tests)
    T_cap <- rep(max(abs(t_obs), na.rm = TRUE), length(t_obs))

  } else if (identical(cap_base, "perm")) {
    # Permutation cap: high quantile of |perm|, inflated by g(n)
    if (is.null(dim(perm_stats))) perm_stats <- matrix(perm_stats, ncol = 1)
    Tabs <- abs(perm_stats)
    Tq <- apply(Tabs, 2, stats::quantile, probs = q_ref, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)
    
    # Inflation g(n) = max(1, alpha * sqrt(n))
    infl <- pmax(1, alpha * sqrt(n))
    T_cap <- Tq * infl

    # Broadcast if needed
    if (length(T_cap) == 1L) T_cap <- rep(T_cap, length(t_obs))
    if (length(T_cap) != length(t_obs)) T_cap <- rep(T_cap[1L], length(t_obs))

  } else {
    stop("cap_base must be 'perm' or 'tmax'.")
  }

  # Guardrails: enforce positive finite caps
  bad <- !is.finite(T_cap) | T_cap <= 0
  if (any(bad)) {
    fallback <- max(T_cap[!bad], 1)
    T_cap[bad] <- fallback
  }
  T_cap
}

# Standardized Lifted Log-Saturation (SLLS) using unified Z-cap
# - Standardize to Z using permutation mean/sd per test
# - Build the cap on the Z-scale via .build_Tcap (cap_base = "perm" or "tmax")
# - Apply simplified LLS on Z
# - Map epsilon back to T-scale via sigma_j
eps_log_saturation_lift_std <- function(obs_stats,
                                        perm_stats,
                                        sampsize,
                                        cap_base      = "perm",  # Z-cap choice
                                        alpha         = 0.5, # g(n) = max(1, alpha*sqrt(n))
                                        q_ref         = 0.99, # quantile of |Z_perm|
                                        rho_lift      = 3,
                                        k_factor      = 1000,
                                        target_factor = 0.25,
                                        floor_epsZ    = 1e-6,
                                        ...) {
  cap_base <- if (is.character(cap_base)) match.arg(cap_base) else cap_base
  n <- sampsize
  stopifnot(n > 0)

  # Shape inputs --------------------------------------------------------------
  # Allow vector perm_stats for single test
  if (is.null(dim(perm_stats))) perm_stats <- matrix(perm_stats, ncol = 1)
  Tobs <- as.numeric(obs_stats)

  # Z-standardization per test -----------------------------------------------
  mu  <- colMeans(perm_stats, na.rm = TRUE)
  sig <- apply(perm_stats, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE)

  # Guard against non-finite or zero SDs: replace with a small positive fallback
  valid_sig <- sig[is.finite(sig) & sig > 0]
  sig_fallback <- if (length(valid_sig)) max(min(valid_sig), 1e-12) else 1e-12
  sig_ok <- sig
  sig_ok[!is.finite(sig_ok) | sig_ok <= 0] <- sig_fallback

  Zobs  <- abs((Tobs - mu) / sig_ok)
  Zperm <- sweep(sweep(perm_stats, 2, mu, "-"), 2, sig_ok, "/")  # same shape as perm_stats

  # Build Z-cap (unified) -----------------------------------------------------
  # Uses: "tmax" => max(Zobs); "perm" => Q_q(|Zperm|) * g(n) with g(n)=max(1, alpha*sqrt(n))
  T_cap_Z <- .build_Tcap(cap_base = cap_base,
                         t_obs    = Zobs,
                         perm_stats = Zperm,
                         n        = n,
                         q_ref    = q_ref,
                         alpha    = alpha)

  # LLS core on Z -------------------------------------------------------------
  k        <- k_factor      * (500 / n)
  E_target <- target_factor * (500 / n)

  if (!any(is.finite(T_cap_Z)) || all(T_cap_Z <= 0)) {
    # Conservative constant if the cap failed
    epsZ <- rep(max(E_target + rho_lift, floor_epsZ), length(Zobs))
  } else {
    s   <- pmin(Zobs / T_cap_Z, 1)        # scale-free index in [0,1]
    psi <- (1 - s)^4 * (1 + 4*s)          # Wendland C^2 lift
    epsZ_core <- E_target * log1p(k * s) / log1p(k) + rho_lift * psi
    epsZ <- pmax(epsZ_core, floor_epsZ)
  }

  # Map back to T-scale -------------------------------------------------------
  epsT <- epsZ * sig_ok
  epsT
}
```

```{r run_permApprox_constr_log_sat_lift_std}
results_all <- run_permApprox(rule_name = "log_sat_lift_std", 
                              eps_fct = eps_log_saturation_lift_std)
```

```{r pvals_constr_log_sat_lift_std}
for (n_val in n_per_group_vec) {
  df_n <- results_all %>% filter(n_per_group == n_val, gpd_fit == TRUE)
  p <- plot_pvals(df = df_n, sampsize = n_val)
  print(p)
}
```

## Files written

These files have been written to the target directory, ```r paste0("data/", params$name)```:

```{r list-files-target}
projthis::proj_dir_info(path_target())
```
